conversation.content:

COLUMNS:

---
Column 1: Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treatment
Definition: What the number and percentage of participants in the treatment arm of the clinical trial with a high volume or burden of metastatic disease? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="497 (76.3%)" | page=5 | modality=table | reasoning="From Table 1, 'High Volumea' for Darolutamide (n = 497). The total number of patients in the Darolutamide arm is 651 (Figure 1), so 497/651 = 76.3%."

| ALP ≥ ULN, No. (%) | 329 (66.2) | 332 (65.4) | 32 (20.8) | 31 (21.2) | 287 (63.5) | 294 (63.9) | 74 (37.2) | 69 (35.6) | 724 (55.5) |



Abbreviations: ADT, androgen-deprivation therapy; ALP, alkaline phosphatase; ECOG PS, Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status; LN, lymph node; mHSPC, metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer; PSA, prostate-specific antigen; ULN, upper level of normal.

*Disease volume defined by CHAARTED criteria: presence of visceral metastases or ≥ 4 bone metastases with ≥ 1 beyond vertebral bodies and pelvis (Sweeney et al).¹

*Disease risk defined by LATITUDE criteria (two of the following three factors): Gleason score of ≥ 8, the presence of ≥ 3 bone lesions, or the presence of measurable visceral metastasis (Fizazi et al).²

*ECOG PS scores range from 0 to 5, with higher scores indicating greater disability.

*Gleason scores for the histologic pattern of carcinoma range from 6 to 10, with higher scores indicating a more aggressive form of prostate cancer.

*One patient had lymph node metastasis alone per direct entry in case report form but was categorized as M1b in the high-volume subgroup using detailed tumor data.

*These values were centrally assessed. Samples were obtained while patients were receiving ADT.
